I think that X option is a bit buggy... An example:

```
[root@es-brick1 x86_64]# cat /etc/modprobe.d/lustre.conf
options libcfs cpu_npartitions=2
options libcfs cpu_pattern="X[0,3]"
[root@es-brick1 x86_64]# lctl get_param cpu_partition_table
cpu_partition_table=
0       : 1 2
1       : 4 5
[root@es-brick1 x86_64]# lustre_rmmod
[root@es-brick1 x86_64]# cat  /etc/modprobe.d/lustre.conf
options libcfs cpu_npartitions=2
options libcfs cpu_pattern="X[0,4]"
[root@es-brick1 x86_64]# modprobe lustre
modprobe: ERROR: could not insert 'lustre': Numerical result out of range

[root@es-brick1 x86_64]# lustre_rmmod
[root@es-brick1 x86_64]# modprobe lustre
modprobe: ERROR: could not insert 'lustre': Numerical result out of range
[root@es-brick1 x86_64]# cat  /etc/modprobe.d/lustre.conf
options libcfs cpu_npartitions=2
options libcfs cpu_pattern="X[1,4]"
```

commit.subject: LU-17501 libcfs: adding X for cpu pattern
commit.message:
LU-17501 libcfs: adding X for cpu pattern

Added 'X' pattern to cpu_pattern such that "X[1]" will remove cpu 1
regardless of what partition it is in.

Using "N X" will set the default NUMA node layout before excluding.
Using "X" without 'N' will set the layout based on npartitions.

This is similarily true for 'C', use 'N' for NUMA layout and not for
an npartitions based layout.
